response:
THE FRAME WEIGHT DECREASES BY TWO PERCENT EACH TIME A NEW PART IS INSTALLED ON THE BIKE.
THE FINAL WEIGHT EQUALS W MULTIPLIED BY ZERO POINT NINE EIGHT TO THE POWER OF N COMPONENTS ADDED.
THE CLOCK SHOWS TIME PASSING AS ALEX CALCULATES THE REQUIRED ENERGY REDUCTION FOR THE LONG RIDE.
THE CLOCK TICKS LOUDLY WHILE JAMIE EXPLAINS THE MATH BEHIND THE PERFORMANCE GAINS ON THE ROAD.
THE CLOCK RUNS FAST WHEN WE SOLVE FOR N TO REACH THE TWENTY FIVE PERCENT TARGET GOAL.
WE NEED TO FIND N WHERE ONE MINUS ZERO POINT NINE EIGHT TO THE POWER N EQUALS ZERO POINT TWO FIVE.
SOLVING THIS EQUATION REVEALS THAT ALEX MUST ADD AT LEAST TWELVE ADVANCED PARTS TO THE BIKE.
THE CLOCK STRIKES TWELVE AS ALEX FINISHES THE CALCULATION AND PREPARES FOR THE EVENT START.
